gene_region.label: KCNJ11 transcribed interval
gene_region.definition: Canonical KCNJ11 genomic interval on GRCh37 / hg19 from Ensembl GRCh37 lookup, aligned to the hg19 coordinate system used by this app.

promoter_review_region.label: Operational promoter review window
promoter_review_region.definition: A practical 1 kb upstream window relative to the reverse-strand transcription start, used to flag promoter-adjacent coverage before the canonical gene interval.

promoter_hotspot_region.label: Promoter-associated CpG review window
promoter_hotspot_region.definition: Promoter-proximal CpG-focused span represented by the bundled EPIC subset and used here as the main methylation-review hotspot.

gene_context.gene_summary: KCNJ11 encodes Kir6.2, the pore-forming subunit of the ATP-sensitive potassium channel. This bundle emphasizes ATP-sensitive potassium channel gating, beta-cell insulin secretion, neonatal diabetes biology, and sulfonylurea pharmacology.
gene_context.clinical_context: The local KCNJ11 bundle is KATP channel and insulin-secretion review oriented. Interpret markers through exact allele class, zygosity, ancestry, phenotype, and assay context rather than as stand-alone deterministic calls.
variant_effect_overview: KCNJ11 variants can alter or tag ATP-sensitive potassium channel gating, beta-cell insulin secretion, neonatal diabetes biology, and sulfonylurea pharmacology; effect direction depends on allele class, tissue, dosage, and biological context.
Common markers, pharmacogenetic alleles, structural events, and high-impact pathogenic variants should be kept separate during interpretation.
gene_context.methylation_interpretation: KCNJ11 methylation should be treated as local regulatory context around a ATP-sensitive potassium channel gating, beta-cell insulin secretion, neonatal diabetes biology, and sulfonylurea pharmacology gene. Promoter-proximal methylation can support expression-potential review, but it should not replace genotype, RNA, protein, or phenotype data.
methylation_effects: Promoter or first-exon methylation may suggest a more restrained or permissive local expression state for KCNJ11.
Because KCNJ11 biology is tissue and pathway dependent, methylation values are context rather than a stand-alone biomarker.

Combined sequence plus methylation findings are most useful when interpreted against ATP-sensitive potassium channel gating, beta-cell insulin secretion, neonatal diabetes biology, and sulfonylurea pharmacology.
methylation_condition_research: Epigenetic regulation studies that use KCNJ11 promoter or gene-body methylation as expression-context evidence.
Variant-plus-methylation review for ATP-sensitive potassium channel gating, beta-cell insulin secretion, neonatal diabetes biology, and sulfonylurea pharmacology.

evidence.label: Ensembl GRCh37 ENSG00000187486: KCNJ11 coordinate source

biorender_visuals.label: KCNJ11 biochemical figure starter
biorender_visuals.focus: KCNJ11: ATP-sensitive potassium channel gating, beta-cell insulin secretion, neonatal diabetes biology, and sulfonylurea pharmacology
recommended_icons: ATP sensitive potassium channel (open, in membrane)
biorender_visuals.usage_note: BioRender template and icon-library search results are bundled as figure starters. Open the template link in BioRender, then add or replace the listed icons to make a gene-specific biochemical pathway image.

gene_context.concrete_variant_prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests a KCNJ11 KATP channel and insulin-secretion review thesis: the individual may carry context relevant to ATP-sensitive potassium channel gating, beta-cell insulin secretion, neonatal diabetes biology, and sulfonylurea pharmacology and Neonatal diabetes review context, Type 2 diabetes association research. Treat this as pathway triage until the exact allele has external classification and phenotype support.

variant_records.clinical_interpretation: rs5219 (KCNJ11 E23K) is curated as a KCNJ11 marker for Neonatal diabetes review context, Type 2 diabetes association research. Read it through genotype dosage, phenotype context, and the ATP-sensitive potassium channel gating, beta-cell insulin secretion, neonatal diabetes biology, and sulfonylurea pharmacology pathway rather than as a stand-alone deterministic result.
variant_records.clinical_significance: Curated research and clinical-context marker; external clinical classification is required for diagnostic use.
functional_effects: Can modify or tag ATP-sensitive potassium channel gating, beta-cell insulin secretion, neonatal diabetes biology, and sulfonylurea pharmacology.
Best interpreted with tissue, ancestry, phenotype, and assay context.
research_context: Use this marker as a KCNJ11 pathway-context signal, not as a stand-alone diagnosis.
Sequence, methylation, expression, and protein data may point in different directions and should be reconciled.
variant_records.methylation_interpretation: Pair rs5219 (KCNJ11 E23K) with KCNJ11 methylation only as local regulatory context; methylation does not prove the variant's biochemical effect.
